SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr

Class LogicalPlanBuilder

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr.LogicalPlanBuilder
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public class LogicalPlanBuilder extends Object implements AutoCloseable
A builder for constructing LogicalPlans programmatically.

Transformation methods consume this builder (the source is closed) and return a new LogicalPlanBuilder, mirroring Rust's ownership model where methods take self. This enables fluent chaining: 

import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

LogicalPlan plan = LogicalPlanBuilder.from(existingPlan, ctx)
    .project(List.of(col("a"), col("b")))
    .filter(col("a").gt(lit(0)))
    .sort(List.of(new SortExpr(col("a"), true, false)))
    .limit(0, 10)
    .build();
DataFrame df = ctx.executeLogicalPlan(plan);
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder

  • Constructor Details

    • LogicalPlanBuilder

      public LogicalPlanBuilder(LogicalPlanBuilderBridge bridge)

  • Method Details

    • empty

      public static LogicalPlanBuilder empty(boolean produceOneRow, SessionContext ctx)
      Creates a builder for an empty relation.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan emptyPlan = LogicalPlanBuilder.empty(false, ctx).build();
      Parameters:
      produceOneRow - if true, the empty relation produces one row with no columns
      ctx - the session context
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::empty

    • from

      public static LogicalPlanBuilder from(LogicalPlan plan, SessionContext ctx)
      Creates a builder from an existing LogicalPlan.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan filtered = LogicalPlanBuilder.from(plan, ctx)
    .filter(col("a").gt(lit(0)))
    .build();
      Parameters:
      plan - the logical plan to start from
      ctx - the session context
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::from

    • project

      public LogicalPlanBuilder project(List<Expr> exprs)
      Projects the specified expressions.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

LogicalPlan plan = builder.project(List.of(col("a"), col("b"))).build();
      Parameters:
      exprs - the expressions to project
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the projection applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::project

    • project

      public LogicalPlanBuilder project(Expr... exprs)
      Projects the specified expressions (varargs convenience).

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Parameters:
      exprs - the expressions to project (see project(List))
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the projection applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::project

    • filter

      public LogicalPlanBuilder filter(Expr predicate)
      Filters rows by the given predicate expression.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

LogicalPlan plan = builder.filter(col("age").gtEq(lit(18))).build();
      Parameters:
      predicate - the filter predicate
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the filter applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::filter

    • sort

      public LogicalPlanBuilder sort(List<SortExpr> sortExprs)
      Sorts by the given sort expressions.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

LogicalPlan plan = builder
    .sort(List.of(new SortExpr(col("name"), true, false)))
    .build();
      Parameters:
      sortExprs - the sort expressions
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the sort applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::sort

    • sort

      public LogicalPlanBuilder sort(SortExpr... sortExprs)
      Sorts by the given sort expressions (varargs convenience).

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Parameters:
      sortExprs - the sort expressions (see sort(List))
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the sort applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::sort

    • limit

      public LogicalPlanBuilder limit(long skip, long fetch)
      Limits the number of rows returned.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan plan = builder.limit(0, 100).build();  // first 100 rows
      Parameters:
      skip - the number of rows to skip
      fetch - the maximum number of rows to return, or -1 for no limit
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the limit applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::limit

    • aggregate

      public LogicalPlanBuilder aggregate(List<Expr> groupExprs, List<Expr> aggrExprs)
      Aggregates with the given group-by and aggregate expressions.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

LogicalPlan plan = builder
    .aggregate(List.of(col("category")), List.of(count(col("id"))))
    .build();
      Parameters:
      groupExprs - the group-by expressions
      aggrExprs - the aggregate expressions
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the aggregation applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::aggregate

    • distinct

      public LogicalPlanBuilder distinct()
      Removes duplicate rows.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan plan = builder.distinct().build();
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the distinct applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::distinct

    • having

      public LogicalPlanBuilder having(Expr predicate)
      Applies a HAVING filter on an aggregation result.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

LogicalPlan plan = builder
    .aggregate(List.of(col("dept")), List.of(count(col("id"))))
    .having(count(col("id")).gt(lit(5)))
    .build();
      Parameters:
      predicate - the having predicate
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the having applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::having

    • window

      public LogicalPlanBuilder window(List<Expr> windowExprs)
      Applies window expressions.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

Expr rowNum = windowFunction("row_number", List.of())
    .partitionBy(List.of(col("dept")))
    .orderBy(List.of(new SortExpr(col("salary"), false, true)))
    .build();
LogicalPlan plan = builder.window(List.of(rowNum)).build();
      Parameters:
      windowExprs - the window expressions
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the window applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::window

    • alias

      public LogicalPlanBuilder alias(String alias)
      Applies a subquery alias.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan plan = builder.alias("t1").build();
      Parameters:
      alias - the alias name
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the alias applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::alias

    • explain

      public LogicalPlanBuilder explain(boolean verbose, boolean analyze)
      Creates an explain plan.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan explained = builder.explain(true, false).build();
      Parameters:
      verbose - if true, include more details
      analyze - if true, actually execute and show timing
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the explain applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::explain

    • join

      public LogicalPlanBuilder join(LogicalPlan right, JoinType joinType, List<String> leftCols, List<String> rightCols)
      Joins with another plan using column name pairs.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan plan = leftBuilder
    .join(rightPlan, JoinType.INNER, List.of("id"), List.of("id"))
    .build();
      Parameters:
      right - the right-side plan to join with
      joinType - the type of join
      leftCols - the left-side join column names
      rightCols - the right-side join column names
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the join applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::join

    • crossJoin

      public LogicalPlanBuilder crossJoin(LogicalPlan right)
      Cross joins with another plan.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan plan = builder.crossJoin(rightPlan).build();
      Parameters:
      right - the right-side plan to cross join with
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the cross join applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::cross_join

    • union

      public LogicalPlanBuilder union(LogicalPlan other)
      Unions with another plan.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan plan = builder.union(otherPlan).build();
      Parameters:
      other - the plan to union with
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the union applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::union

    • unionDistinct

      public LogicalPlanBuilder unionDistinct(LogicalPlan other)
      Unions with another plan, removing duplicates.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan plan = builder.unionDistinct(otherPlan).build();
      Parameters:
      other - the plan to union distinct with
      Returns:
      a new LogicalPlanBuilder with the union distinct applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::union_distinct

    • intersect

      public static LogicalPlan intersect(LogicalPlan left, LogicalPlan right, boolean isAll)
      Intersects two plans.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan result = LogicalPlanBuilder.intersect(leftPlan, rightPlan, false);
      Parameters:
      left - the left-side plan
      right - the right-side plan
      isAll - if true, INTERSECT ALL (keeps duplicates); if false, INTERSECT
      Returns:
      the intersected LogicalPlan
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::intersect

    • except

      public static LogicalPlan except(LogicalPlan left, LogicalPlan right, boolean isAll)
      Computes the set difference of two plans.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan result = LogicalPlanBuilder.except(leftPlan, rightPlan, false);
      Parameters:
      left - the left-side plan
      right - the right-side plan
      isAll - if true, EXCEPT ALL (keeps duplicates); if false, EXCEPT
      Returns:
      the excepted LogicalPlan
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::except

    • build

      public LogicalPlan build()
      Builds and returns the final LogicalPlan.

      This is a consuming method: the source builder is closed after the call.

      Example: 

      LogicalPlan plan = builder.build();
DataFrame df = ctx.executeLogicalPlan(plan);
      Returns:
      the built LogicalPlan
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: LogicalPlanBuilder::build

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
